pr.title: Match more functions and data in `ftCo_Damage`
pr.author: mchen91

summary: Matched additional ftCo_Damage code and associated sdata2 constants, specifically around damage fly animation/physics, damage fly roll animation/physics, knockback steering, and damage transition handling. The PR converted several anonymous lbl_804D* constants to compiler-style @NNN float/double symbols and reshaped C control flow, temporaries, stack padding, inlining boundaries, and GET_FIGHTER usage to improve matching.
what_changed: Matched functions listed in the PR body: ftCo_DamageFly_Anim, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim, ftCo_DamageFly_Phys, ftCo_8008E5A4, and ftCo_8008E9D0.

Renamed several sdata2 labels in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from lbl_804D84D0/lbl_804D84E0/lbl_804D84E8/lbl_804D84EC/lbl_804D84F0/lbl_804D84F8/lbl_804DA718 to compiler-style @216, @220, @320, @321, @460, @521, and @637.

Restructured ftCo_8008E5A4 so knockback steering calculations use fewer nested scopes, explicit f3/f30 temporaries, PSVECCrossProduct sign handling, and a separate scale variable before adjusting atan2f-derived angle.
Changed inlineF0 to obtain the fighter via GET_FIGHTER(gobj), removed an extra block, and preserved motion_id checks that call ftCo_800DC284 for 0xe0/0xe1 and ftCo_800DC3A4 for 0xe3/0xe4.

Updated ftCo_8008E9D0 to remove an unused stack byte array, introduce scaled_kb = ftCo_ScaleBy154(fp->dmg.kb_applied), and call ftCo_8008DA4C(gobj, fp->dmg.x1860_element, ftCo_8008D8E8(scaled_kb)) instead of the prior argument pattern.
In ftCo_8008EC90, simplified one branch by calling ftCo_8008E9D0(gobj) unconditionally where the old source guarded it with inlineB0(gobj).

Replaced direct gobj->user_data fighter loads with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in several helpers and callbacks including ftCo_8008F744, inlineC0, ftCo_Damage_Anim, inlineD0, ftCo_DamageFly_Anim, doFlyRoll, ftCo_DamageFly_Phys, ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim, and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys.
Wrapped ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, indicating the matching depended on preventing inlining at that point.

Reworked inlineD0 to guard on fp->mv.co.damage.x8 != 0 before decrementing, add u8 _[12] stack padding, pass &trajectory directly to efAsync_Spawn, and call ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold only after the timer reaches zero.
Added a new static inlineE0 helper to share the x221C_b6-based air physics choice between ftCo_DamageFly_Phys and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys.
Changed ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim to use PAD_STACK(8), always call inlineD0(gobj), and rely on inlineD0's internal x8 guard rather than checking fp->mv.co.damage.x8 at the call site.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated airborne damage-fly physics branch into inlineE0, reducing duplicate source while still preserving the apparent compiler shape used by both DamageFly_Phys and DamageFlyRoll_Phys.
Moved the mv.co.damage.x8 guard into inlineD0, which allowed callers such as ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim to call it unconditionally while maintaining behavior and improving match shape.
Used #pragma dont_inline around ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold to force the compiler boundary needed by the matched output instead of relying on fragile incidental inlining decisions.

Switched many fighter pointer fetches from gobj->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), matching project idiom and likely improving generated code consistency where the macro expands to the expected access pattern.
Replaced ad hoc dummy stack arrays with PAD_STACK(8) where appropriate, while retaining explicit stack padding in inlineD0 where the diff suggests that exact stack layout was still needed.
Adjusted data symbol names to @NNN local/compiler-generated constant labels, which documents that these sdata2 entries are compiler literals rather than meaningful global objects.
decomp_lessons: For Melee decomp matching, behaviorally equivalent source may need careful reshaping of temporary variable lifetime, scope nesting, and expression ordering to reproduce the target assembly.
Small changes to where a guard lives can matter: moving the x8 nonzero check into inlineD0 changed call-site structure while preserving semantic intent.
Shared inline helpers can be useful matching tools when two functions contain identical branch logic; inlineE0 captures the x221C_b6 air-physics choice once for both damage fly variants.
Use compiler-style @NNN names for unnamed float/double constants when evidence suggests they are literal pool entries, not semantically named globals.
Explicit pragmas such as #pragma dont_inline are acceptable matching tools when a function must remain out-of-line.
Stack layout remains important in this codebase: the diff shows both removal of obsolete dummy arrays and introduction/retention of PAD_STACK or u8 padding where needed.
naming_conventions: Unknown compiler-generated sdata2 constants are represented as @NNN symbols, for example @216, @220, @320, @321, @460, @521, and @637.
Fighter objects are preferably retrieved with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in matched ftCommon code when that produces the expected source/assembly shape.
Inline helper names in this file continue the temporary decomp convention inlineB0, inlineC0, inlineD0, inlineE0, inlineF0 when exact semantic names are not yet established.

Existing ftCommon damage naming remains address-based for unknown functions such as ftCo_8008E5A4, ftCo_8008E9D0, ftCo_8008EC90, and ftCo_8008F744, while known callbacks use names like ftCo_DamageFly_Anim and ftCo_DamageFly_Phys.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced explicit temporaries f3, f30, scale, angle, kb_mag, and Vec3 locals in ftCo_8008E5A4 to control float operation ordering around atan2f, sqrtf, cosf, sinf, and PSVECCrossProduct.
Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop to prevent ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold from being inlined.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim instead of manual dummy arrays.
Used explicit u8 _[12] stack padding in inlineD0, showing that manual stack shaping was still necessary there.
Passed &trajectory directly to efAsync_Spawn in inlineD0 instead of assigning through a separate param variable.
Removed stale dummy stack arrays from ftCo_8008E9D0, ftCo_Damage_Anim, ftCo_DamageFly_Phys, and ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys where no longer needed for the match.
Changed ftCo_8008DA4C argument construction in ftCo_8008E9D0 to use fp->dmg.x1860_element and ftCo_8008D8E8(ftCo_ScaleBy154(fp->dmg.kb_applied)), matching the revised call shape.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text excerpt is the PR description listing the matched functions and data.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftCo_8008E5A4, ftCo_8008E9D0, ftCo_8008EC90, or ftCo_8008F744 with semantic names.
Check whether later ftCo_Damage work removes temporary inline helper names inlineD0/inlineE0/inlineF0 or replaces them with named static helpers.
Search for follow-up changes to ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold to see whether the dont_inline pragma remained necessary.
Compare subsequent symbols.txt changes around @216, @220, @320, @321, @460, @521, and @637 for more meaningful constant identification.
